definition: When price attempts to take out a swing high/low but FAILS to close beyond it — the wick sweeps but the body cannot hold. This signals exhaustion and likely reversal.
depends_on: liquidity (SFP IS a failed liquidity sweep — stops are triggered but the move can't sustain)
feeds_into: entry_models (SFP itself is an entry signal)
market_structure (SFP at a key swing creates a potential CHoCH)
related_to: turtle_soup (SFP is conceptually similar — both are failed breakouts)
liquidity_sweep (SFP is a sweep that FAILS to follow through)
rejection_blocks (the SFP candle's wick zone = rejection block)
vs_turtle_soup: Turtle soup: price breaks through and closes back inside = confirmed reversal. SFP: price can't even close beyond = exhaustion signal. SFP is WEAKER confirmation than turtle soup but fires earlier.
buy_SFP: Price wicks below a prior swing low but closes above it. Indicates buying pressure absorbed the sell-side raid.
sell_SFP: Price wicks above a prior swing high but closes below it. Indicates selling pressure absorbed the buy-side raid.
how_to_use: SFP at a HTF key level (PDH/PDL, OB, FVG boundary) = high-conviction reversal signal. SFP at a random swing = lower significance.

bridge_integration.detection: Scanned via swing_failure_pattern check: candle wicks through prior swing but body closes back inside. Flagged in confluence_factors.
bridge_integration.prompt_display: SFP adds to sweep confluence. Noted as 'failed breakout' signal in analysis context for Claude.
bridge_integration.score_impact: SFP at PDH/PDL or session level = +4 reversal confluence. SFP confirming existing sweep = cumulative sweep score boost.
